prerequisites: A running IBM Db2 instance reachable from the connector, with its host and port (the connector's host and port fields).
The target database name and the schemas to replicate (the database and schemas fields).

A Db2 user with read access to the schemas you want to replicate. The vendor docs recommend creating a dedicated read-only role and user, for example: CREATE ROLE AIRBYTE_ROLE; GRANT CONNECT ON DATABASE TO ROLE AIRBYTE_ROLE; GRANT ROLE AIRBYTE_ROLE TO USER AIRBYTE_USER.
To use a TLS/SSL connection, a client certificate in PEM format for the encryption.ssl_certificate field.
To reach Db2 over an SSH bastion, network access to the SSH host plus either an SSH private key (tunnel_method.ssh_key) or the SSH user's password (tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password).

authMethods.name: Username and password (JDBC)
authMethods.summary: Primary authentication. The connector logs in to Db2 over JDBC with the username field and the password secret field. Use a dedicated read-only Db2 user granted CONNECT and access to the schemas being replicated.

authMethods.name: TLS/SSL client certificate
authMethods.summary: Optional transport encryption configured through the encryption config object. Provide a client certificate in PEM format in the encryption.ssl_certificate secret field; the connector adds it to its keystore for the SSL connection.

authMethods.name: SSH tunnel (key or password)
authMethods.summary: Optional connectivity through an SSH bastion, selected via the tunnel_method config object. Authenticate to the SSH host with an SSH private key (tunnel_method.ssh_key) or with the SSH user's password (tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password). Db2 username and password auth still applies inside the tunnel.

setupSteps.title: Create a read-only Db2 user
setupSteps.body: In Db2, create a dedicated user and role for replication and grant it read access to the target schemas. The vendor docs give: CREATE ROLE AIRBYTE_ROLE; GRANT CONNECT ON DATABASE TO ROLE AIRBYTE_ROLE; GRANT ROLE AIRBYTE_ROLE TO USER AIRBYTE_USER. This user maps to the connector's username and password fields.

setupSteps.title: Provide connection details
setupSteps.body: Enter the required host, port, and database fields so the connector can reach the Db2 instance, then set username and password for JDBC authentication. List the schemas to replicate in the schemas field and set the cursor field used to track incremental sync position.

setupSteps.title: Choose an encryption mode
setupSteps.body: Configure the required encryption object. For an unencrypted connection leave it as such; for TLS/SSL, supply a PEM-format client certificate in encryption.ssl_certificate, which the connector loads into its keystore.

setupSteps.title: Configure the SSH tunnel (if needed)
setupSteps.body: Set the required tunnel_method object. Choose no tunnel for direct access, or route through an SSH bastion by authenticating with an SSH private key (tunnel_method.ssh_key) or the SSH user's password (tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password).

setupSteps.title: Tune optional settings
setupSteps.body: Optionally set jdbc_url_params to append extra JDBC connection parameters, check_privileges to verify table read access during discovery, and concurrency, checkpoint_target_interval_seconds, and related fields to control sync behavior.
